# OpenCLI API Guides
<!-- Generated by Copilot -->

This document provides practical guides and examples for using the OpenCLI API.

## Introduction

The OpenCLI API provides a RESTful interface to programmatically manage your OpenPanel installation. All endpoints require authentication using API tokens.

## Authentication

Before using any API endpoint, you need to generate an API token:

```bash
# Generate a token for your user
opencli api-token-generate

# Generate a token for another user (admin only)
opencli api-token-generate --user username --expiry 90
```

Include the token in all API requests using the Authorization header:

```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN
```

## Common Use Cases

### Automating User Management

You can automate user onboarding by creating scripts that:

1. Create a new user
2. Add initial domains
3. Install specific PHP versions
4. Configure resources based on templates

Example script:

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Configuration
API_TOKEN="your_api_token"
API_BASE="https://panel.example.com/api/v1"
USERNAME="newclient"
PASSWORD="SecureP@ssw0rd"
EMAIL="newclient@example.com"
PLAN="basic"
DOMAINS=("example.com" "example.org")
PHP_VERSIONS=("7.4" "8.2")

# Create user
USER_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST "$API_BASE/users" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"username\":\"$USERNAME\",\"password\":\"$PASSWORD\",\"email\":\"$EMAIL\",\"plan\":\"$PLAN\"}")

echo "Created user: $USER_RESPONSE"

# Add domains
for domain in "${DOMAINS[@]}"; do
  DOMAIN_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST "$API_BASE/domains" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"domain\":\"$domain\",\"user\":\"$USERNAME\"}")

  echo "Added domain $domain: $DOMAIN_RESPONSE"
done

# Install PHP versions
for version in "${PHP_VERSIONS[@]}"; do
  PHP_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST "$API_BASE/php/install" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"username\":\"$USERNAME\",\"version\":\"$version\"}")

  echo "Installed PHP $version: $PHP_RESPONSE"
done
```

### Backup Management

You can create a script to manage regular backups:

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Configuration
API_TOKEN="your_api_token"
API_BASE="https://panel.example.com/api/v1"
USERNAME="client1"

# List existing backups
BACKUPS=$(curl -s -X GET "$API_BASE/backups/$USERNAME" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN")

# Count backups
BACKUP_COUNT=$(echo $BACKUPS | jq '.backups | length')
echo "User has $BACKUP_COUNT backups"

# Create a new backup if count is less than 3
if [ $BACKUP_COUNT -lt 3 ]; then
  NEW_BACKUP=$(curl -s -X POST "$API_BASE/backups" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"username\":\"$USERNAME\",\"type\":\"full\"}")

  echo "Created new backup: $NEW_BACKUP"
fi
```

### Plans Management

You can automate plan creation and management:

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Configuration
API_TOKEN="your_api_token"
API_BASE="https://panel.example.com/api/v1"

# Create different plans
PLANS=(
  '{"name":"starter","description":"Starter Plan","email_limit":5,"ftp_limit":3,"domains_limit":5,"websites_limit":3,"disk_limit":10,"inodes_limit":250000,"db_limit":5,"cpu":1,"ram":1,"docker_image":"nginx","bandwidth":500}'
  '{"name":"business","description":"Business Plan","email_limit":20,"ftp_limit":10,"domains_limit":20,"websites_limit":10,"disk_limit":50,"inodes_limit":500000,"db_limit":20,"cpu":2,"ram":4,"docker_image":"nginx","bandwidth":1000}'
  '{"name":"enterprise","description":"Enterprise Plan","email_limit":100,"ftp_limit":50,"domains_limit":100,"websites_limit":50,"disk_limit":200,"inodes_limit":2000000,"db_limit":100,"cpu":4,"ram":16,"docker_image":"nginx","bandwidth":3000}'
)

# Create each plan
for plan_json in "${PLANS[@]}"; do
  PLAN_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST "$API_BASE/plans" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "$plan_json")

  echo "Created plan: $PLAN_RESPONSE"
done
```

### System Monitoring and Management

You can build monitoring dashboards and automate system management:

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Configuration
API_TOKEN="your_api_token"
API_BASE="https://panel.example.com/api/v1"

# Get system status
SYSTEM_STATUS=$(curl -s -X GET "$API_BASE/system/status" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN")

# Extract CPU and memory usage
CPU_USAGE=$(echo $SYSTEM_STATUS | jq '.cpu.usage_percentage')
MEM_USAGE=$(echo $SYSTEM_STATUS | jq '.memory.usage_percentage')
DISK_USAGE=$(echo $SYSTEM_STATUS | jq '.disk.usage_percentage')

echo "System Status:"
echo "CPU: $CPU_USAGE%"
echo "Memory: $MEM_USAGE%"
echo "Disk: $DISK_USAGE%"

# Check if any service is stopped
OPENPANEL_STATUS=$(echo $SYSTEM_STATUS | jq -r '.services.openpanel')
ADMIN_STATUS=$(echo $SYSTEM_STATUS | jq -r '.services.admin')
MYSQL_STATUS=$(echo $SYSTEM_STATUS | jq -r '.services.mysql')
NGINX_STATUS=$(echo $SYSTEM_STATUS | jq -r '.services.nginx')

# Restart any stopped service
if [ "$OPENPANEL_STATUS" = "stopped" ]; then
  echo "OpenPanel service is stopped. Restarting..."
  curl -s -X POST "$API_BASE/system/services" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"service":"openpanel","action":"restart"}'
fi

if [ "$ADMIN_STATUS" = "stopped" ]; then
  echo "Admin service is stopped. Restarting..."
  curl -s -X POST "$API_BASE/system/services" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"service":"admin","action":"restart"}'
fi
```

## API Request/Response Examples

### List Domains

Request:
```
GET /api/v1/domains?user=client1 HTTP/1.1
Host: panel.example.com
Authorization: Bearer your_api_token
```

Response:
```json
{
  "domains": [
    {
      "name": "example.com",
      "user": "client1",
      "created_at": "2023-01-15 14:30:22",
      "status": "active"
    },
    {
      "name": "test.com",
      "user": "client1",
      "created_at": "2023-02-20 09:12:45",
      "status": "active"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Install PHP Version

Request:
```
POST /api/v1/php/install HTTP/1.1
Host: panel.example.com
Authorization: Bearer your_api_token
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "username": "client1",
  "version": "8.2"
}
```

Response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "PHP 8.2 has been installed for user client1"
}
```

### Create a Backup

Request:
```
POST /api/v1/backups HTTP/1.1
Host: panel.example.com
Authorization: Bearer your_api_token
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "username": "client1",
  "type": "full"
}
```

Response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Backup for user client1 has been initiated",
  "backup_id": "backup_client1_20230615_143322"
}
```

### Check System Status

Request:
```
GET /api/v1/system/status HTTP/1.1
Host: panel.example.com
Authorization: Bearer your_api_token
```

Response:
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "cpu": {
    "usage_percentage": 24.5
  },
  "memory": {
    "total": "16384 MB",
    "used": "8192 MB",
    "usage_percentage": 50
  },
  "disk": {
    "used": "46080 MB",
    "available": "56320 MB",
    "usage_percentage": 45
  },
  "services": {
    "openpanel": "running",
    "admin": "running",
    "mysql": "running",
    "nginx": "running"
  },
  "version": "1.2.3",
  "uptime": "2 days, 5 hours, 30 minutes"
}
```

### Restart a Service

Request:
```
POST /api/v1/system/services HTTP/1.1
Host: panel.example.com
Authorization: Bearer your_api_token
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "service": "openpanel",
  "action": "restart"
}
```

Response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Service openpanel restart operation completed successfully"
}
```

## Error Handling

All API endpoints return appropriate HTTP status codes:

- 200 OK: Request was successful
- 400 Bad Request: Invalid parameters
- 401 Unauthorized: Missing or invalid token
- 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions
- 404 Not Found: Requested resource doesn't exist
- 500 Internal Server Error: Server-side error

Error responses include error details:

```json
{
  "error": "User not found",
  "status": 404
}
```

## Rate Limiting

The API implements rate limiting to prevent abuse. You might receive a 429 Too Many Requests status code if you exceed the limits.
